_comment: Firefight v9.0.2 map classifications. 27 maps total, hand-classified by visual inspection of 384x384 briefing thumbnails (-map.jpg). See AUDIT_INTEGRATION.md for context. Source: C:/Users/User/Downloads/Firefight.v9.0.2/Firefight.v9.0.2/Maps/. NOTE: D7 extrapolation formula in AUDIT_INTEGRATION.md is superseded — we have 4 real urban_sparse exemplars, no need to extrapolate.

BGHL.notes: dense tree canopy with scattered clearings

BLNU.notes: scattered residential buildings in rural setting

DGCC.notes: grid street layout with big industrial/commercial buildings

FLAU.notes: massive parking lots + warehouses, minimal green space

H112.notes: small village cluster with surrounding fields and trees

JLRD.notes: dense dark green canopy, river valley

JWVG.notes: scattered residential plots in forested area, village feel

KMPV.notes: very dense dark tree coverage, minimal clearing

KRDG.notes: open fields with scattered tree clusters, hedgerows

LBCH.notes: dense tree coverage with winding water channel

MCVL.notes: predominantly open fields with scattered small tree clusters

MLKS.notes: balanced mix of fields, forest patches, water

NSPN.notes: scattered buildings in heavily forested suburban area

RMLY.notes: dense forest with winding river, minimal development

ROIM.notes: scattered suburban buildings in forested landscape

SRVR.notes: large solar farm array dominates, geometric pattern

STPA.notes: agricultural fields with scattered trees, hedgerows

TRCH.notes: village settlement in mixed rural/forested terrain

VBOC.notes: dense street grid with multiple building blocks

VJCT.notes: scattered buildings amid forest, junction cluster

ZCRD.notes: village with distinct settlement pattern in forested area

arid_strategy._comment: No Firefight arid exemplar exists (WW2 settings are European/Pacific). Arid panel targets authored manually against real-world analogues and left at 3.0x tolerance. Parked as a second-tier biome per user: 'Fuck arid i dont have a good example of it for you. We'll want it later.'
arid_strategy.justification: No Firefight ground truth. Allow coarse parity until we source an arid reference (e.g., from Spec Ops: The Line or a Sahara-theater game) post-MVP.

urban_sparse_strategy._comment: D7 of AUDIT_INTEGRATION.md proposed extrapolation (urban_dense*0.4 + rural_village*0.6). That was based on the incorrect assumption that no urban_sparse exemplars existed. Superseded: we have 4 direct exemplars (BLNU, NSPN, ROIM, VJCT), no extrapolation needed.
